captions.humorous_non_tech: Korea Media Production sign just stands there watching the chaos like a disappointed dad at a Little League game. The golden ginkgo trees in the median are absolutely serving main character energy while thousands of cars rush past them to nowhere. A bright orange taxi weaves through traffic like it's personally offended by the concept of lanes. The white delivery truck with red accents shows up and suddenly every sedan remembers they have somewhere else to be. Pedestrians in dark clothing inch across the white-striped crosswalk with the urgency of people who've already accepted they'll be late. Brake lights start flashing red in the far lanes because of course the universe said "nobody asked for this but here we are" to everyone trying to leave. The pale hazy sky just keeps being pale and hazy, completely unbothered by the entire city's collective road rage.
captions.formal: Golden late-afternoon light washes over a wide multi-lane thoroughfare in a Korean city, illuminating rows of identical beige and grey apartment windows and a prominent mid-ground sign reading "KOREA MEDIA PRODUCTION" in dark lettering against its pale facade. A central median of vibrant golden-yellow ginkgo trees glows intensely between streams of traffic, while lush green and yellow trees line the right sidewalk and partially obscure the buildings behind them. White sedans, silver hatchbacks, a bright orange taxi, and a large white bus flow continuously in both directions across the dark grey asphalt, their speeds creating a visual rhythm of sharp foreground blurs against slower distant clusters. A large boxy white delivery truck with red accents pushes through the lanes midway through, its bulk dwarfing the smaller passenger cars weaving around it, including a bright red car and a dark blue SUV that flash against the grey road. Pedestrians in dark clothing move slowly along the sidewalks, their deliberate pace contrasting with the rushing vehicles, while a few small figures wait at a white-striped crosswalk in the lower left. Traffic thickens noticeably in the lanes heading away from the camera as the sequence progresses, a white sedan and dark grey minivan clustering together with intermittent red brake lights flaring. The pale hazy sky softens the shadows lengthening across the road, and the "KOREA MEDIA PRODUCTION" sign remains a fixed anchor as the golden light deepens, capturing the energetic yet serene pulse of urban life at day's edge.
captions.sarcastic: A sea of beige and grey apartment blocks rises in smug uniformity behind a road that someone clearly decided needed more lanes than purpose, all under a sky the color of a blank Word document. Golden ginkgo trees stand in the median like decorative plastic sushi, glowing aggressively as if photosynthesis is a competitive sport they are determined to win. Cars in white, silver, and one desperate orange taxi surge back and forth beneath the permanently smug "KOREA MEDIA PRODUCTION" sign, which presumably produces content about exactly this kind of traffic. A boxy white delivery truck with red accents muscles through the lanes in the middle sequence, because even logistics deserves its moment of aesthetic disruption among the sedans. Pedestrians in dark clothing creep along the sidewalks at approximately one-tenth the speed of their metal counterparts, presumably calculating how many more years of this before they too buy a white sedan. By the end, brake lights flash red in the lanes heading away from the camera, a synchronized visual poem about the inevitable physics of too many vehicles and too little destination. The warm afternoon light stretches everything into soft shadows, making the entire congestion look almost like it was planned, which is the most impressive production of all.
captions.humorous_tech: Our deployment pipeline is live from the KOREA MEDIA PRODUCTION sign downward, where the green traffic lights are basically the only thing passing code review right now. The white sedan in the foreground is my unit test that "works on my machine" but absolutely fails in production, while the orange taxi cutting left is a hotfix pushed directly to main at 4:47 PM. That boxy white delivery truck with red accents? Classic O(n²) algorithm trying to merge into a lane that clearly has a race condition with the dark blue SUV. The golden-yellow ginkgo trees in the median are just sitting there like Stack Overflow tabs I refuse to close even though I've already solved the problem. Watch the cluster of brake lights heading away from the camera—that's every developer realizing the Friday deploy they rubber-ducked through just hit a NullPointerException in the wild. The pedestrians in dark clothing crossing lower left are the QA team, moving slow and deliberate because they saw this chaos in staging and nobody listened. By final frame, the white bus and grey minivan are locked in a deadlock so beautiful it belongs on a pull request nobody wants to review, golden light casting long shadows like the technical debt we keep deprioritizing. The atmosphere is energetic yet serene because we've all accepted that production is just staging with better traffic lights and worse logging.

captions.sarcastic: A tiny orange tabby kitten with amber eyes and a pink nose sits motionless behind a pale sapling in a sun-dappled clearing of sandy earth and green foliage, as if its camouflage strategy is fooling anyone. The creature then lifts its right front paw with the theatrical caution of someone defusing a bomb, placing it onto the loose dirt while its body stays low to the ground in a posture that screams "I have no idea what I am doing." The kitten advances between the slender trunks, the dappled sunlight dancing across its darker orange striping as the sapling that once obscured its left side drifts behind it like a forgotten prop. The key moment arrives when the kitten locks its amber eyes onto the lens and commits to walking directly toward the camera with increasing steadiness, each paw placed with growing confidence that surely no predator could possibly be hiding inside this black mechanical device. Its fluffy cream chest and delicate white whiskers become fully visible as the background dissolves into a soft blur of green leaves, the kitten apparently deciding that personal space is a myth invented by cowards. In the final frames, the kitten grows larger and larger in the frame while its previously hidden tail emerges upright, signaling the relaxed confidence of a creature that has never once considered the possibility of consequences. The atmosphere shifts from quiet observation to a gentle engagement, as if we are all supposed to be moved by the bold journey of a predator the size of a tennis ball crossing three feet of dirt.
captions.humorous_non_tech: A tiny orange tabby kitten with cream chest fur and a pink nose sits frozen behind a pale-barked sapling like it's waiting for its Uber Eats to arrive in this sun-dappled forest clearing. Those amber eyes are locked on the camera with the intensity of someone who just heard their name in a group chat. Then the right front paw lifts—slowly, dramatically, like this sandy patch of earth is a minefield of questionable life choices. The kitten's body stays low, swaying between the green leafy branches, main character energy fully activated despite being approximately four inches tall. Here comes the key moment: confidence kicks in, the left paw follows the right, and that thin sapling slides behind its fluffy back like a curtain opening on Broadway. The tail pops up, upright and relaxed, because apparently walking twelve inches on tan dirt is a personal growth journey now. By the end, this golden-furred rookie is practically in your lap, whiskers forward, dappled sunlight glowing off its striping like nature's own ring light. Nobody asked for this level of emotional investment before noon, yet here we are, completely owned by a creature smaller than a croissant.
captions.humorous_tech: A lone orange tabby kitten with cream paws and amber eyes sits frozen behind a pale sapling, executing what can only be described as a production hotfix at 3 AM—motionless, praying nobody notices the deployment. The dappled sunlight filtering through green leaves casts jittery shadows across the sandy patch, like a CI pipeline flashing yellow warnings that everyone ignores. Then the right front paw lifts: tentative, deliberate, the first unit test passing in a suite nobody trusted to run. The kitten inches forward between slender trunks, its body low to the tan earth, navigating merge conflicts with the surrounding foliage that rustle like angry Stack Overflow comments. The key commit lands when confidence builds—left paw follows right, the sapling slips behind its flank, and those amber eyes lock onto the lens with the intensity of a senior dev reviewing your pull request at 4:47 PM on a Friday. The tail emerges upright, a green build status finally achieved after seventeen failed runs. The atmosphere shifts from cautious observation to warm connection, like rubber duck debugging that actually worked and you're not sure whether to celebrate or question your entire career. The kitten advances steadily larger in frame, soft orange fur swallowing the lens, a feature shipped directly to prod that somehow, impossibly, works on every machine.
captions.formal: Sunlight filters through a canopy of fresh green leaves and pale-barked saplings, casting dappled shadows across the sandy tan earth of a dense woodland thicket at bright midday. A small orange tabby kitten with amber eyes, a pink nose, cream-coloured chest fur, and darker orange striping along its back sits motionless behind a thin vertical sapling that partially hides its left side. The kitten lifts its right front paw and places it forward onto the loose dirt, then continues with hesitant, low-to-the-ground steps between the slender trunks. Its amber eyes lock onto the camera lens with unwavering focus as the sapling passes behind its body, revealing delicate white whiskers framing its pink nose. The kitten sways gently with each forward step, right paw followed by left, growing steadily larger in the frame as it advances. Its tail rises upright in relaxed confidence, signalling growing comfort with the unfamiliar terrain. The low, ground-level perspective maintains intimate eye contact with the approaching feline until the clip ends with the kitten nearly filling the frame, the background dissolved into a soft blur of green leaves and pale branches.

Circular white pendant lights cast cool, even illumination across a modern open-plan office where crisp white desks and glass partition walls define the space. A young Black woman in her twenties sits in profile at a white desk, her voluminous natural afro puff gathered high on her crown, wearing an unbuttoned light beige overshirt over a bright coral-orange ribbed top with a delicate gold cross necklace resting against her chest. Her vivid pink fingernails rest lightly on a white keyboard as she stares intently at a large black monitor, her shoulders squared and lips pressed in concentrated scrutiny. Behind her, broad green leaves of a potted peace lily punctuate the muted palette of whites, greys, and blacks, while a blurred figure in a blue shirt stands near a coat rack in the background. Her gaze drops slightly downward, her lips part, and her right hand begins moving with purpose as bright pink nails catch the overhead light, pressing keys in a measured, deliberate rhythm. The medium close-up holds steady on her left profile, capturing soft shadows cast by her hair against the geometric lines of the sleek black monitor and the static, leafy presence behind her.
captions.sarcastic: A young Black woman with a voluminous natural afro puff sits beneath circular white pendant lights in a sterile office of crisp whites and cool greys, her coral-orange ribbed top practically screaming for attention against the beige overshirt she left unbuttoned. She stares at a black monitor with the focused intensity of someone watching paint dry, her bright pink fingernails hovering over a white keyboard like they have somewhere better to be. The peace lily behind her stands as the only living thing that has not surrendered to the muted black cables snaking across her desk. Her shoulders remain squared in what can only be described as determined resignation, a gold cross necklace resting against her chest as if offering spiritual protection from spreadsheet hell. Then her gaze drops downward, her lips part with the thrill of silent mouthing, and her right hand begins pressing keys in a measured rhythm that suggests data entry is the highlight of her Tuesday. The black computer mouse sits untouched to her left, presumably as devastated by the typing as the rest of us. Somewhere in the background, a blurred figure in blue stands near a coat rack, perhaps contemplating escape. The cool, even illumination continues to cast its indifferent light upon this tableau of modern productivity, stud earrings and all.
captions.humorous_tech: She's staring at that black monitor like it's a production outage at 3 AM and the on-call rotation just landed on her. The coral-orange ribbed top under her beige overshirt is basically the only warning colour in this entire white-and-grey office that screams "something is definitely on fire." You can see the exact moment her gaze drops downward—probably checking Stack Overflow on a second screen or verifying that the fix actually works on her machine before she dares to commit. Her pink fingernails start hitting those white keys with the deliberate rhythm of someone writing a pull request description that carefully explains why the previous developer's O(n²) solution had to die. The peace lily behind her is the only thing thriving in this environment, probably because it's the only codebase here that hasn't been touched by twenty different contractors. She's not frantically typing—she's rubber duck debugging through a race condition that only appears in production, methodically pressing each key like she's negotiating with a NullPointerException that refuses to reproduce locally. The tangle of black cables across her desk is just the physical manifestation of her team's deployment pipeline, and that blue-shirted figure lingering near the coat rack is definitely waiting to ask why her unit tests passed but staging still exploded.
captions.humorous_non_tech: She's sitting in an office so aggressively white that even the circular pendant lights look like they’re judging her outfit choices. The beige overshirt over coral-orange is giving “I’m approachable but I will absolutely audit your lunch breaks,” and that gold cross necklace is praying for whoever just emailed her. The peace lily behind her is the only living thing in this room that isn't performing stress, and frankly it's thriving on her tension. She starts with that narrowed-eyes, lips-pressed-together stare at the black monitor like the screen personally owes her money. Then her gaze drops downward, her lips part, and those bright pink fingernails start hitting the white keyboard with the measured rhythm of someone composing a very polite but devastatingly firm message. The blue-shirted blur in the background near the coat rack is wisely staying there, probably calculating if he can leave without making eye contact. She’s not typing fast, she’s typing righteously, and every deliberate keystroke says “per my last email” in a color that demands to be seen. The cables snaking across her desk are the only thing in this room more tangled than her patience, and none of us were ready for this level of quiet office power.
